• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Card (1995) — Outcome Weights & Kappa Estimators

College Proximity Instrument: SUW Table 3 Replication + Translation Invariance

Alexander Unger

09 June 2026

Setup

Packages & Functions

library(haven)
library(AER)
library(sandwich)
library(lmtest)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# All estimators, analytical SEs, weight diagnostics — sourced from the shared
# library. Identical functions to the Vietnam (Angrist 1990) replication.
source("functions_all.R")
cat("functions_all.R sourced.\n")
## functions_all.R sourced.

Data & Design

Setting. Card (1995) NLSYM subsample (N = 3,010). Instrument Z = nearc4 (grew up near a 4-year college). Two binary treatments: somecol = 1{educ > 12} (some college) and educ16 = 1{educ ≥ 16} (college completion). Outcome: log wages in two units (dollars / cents) to test translation invariance. Two covariate specifications: Card (exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg661–668) and Kitagawa (black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66).

data_path  <- "/Users/alexung/Desktop/master_thesis/code/"
card       <- read_dta(file.path(data_path, "card.dta"))

# Sample restriction: N = 3,010
card_clean <- card[!is.na(card$wage) & !is.na(card$educ), ]

# Outcomes in two units: lwage_cnt = lwage + log(100)
card_clean$lwage_cnt <- log(card_clean$wage)         # cents
card_clean$lwage     <- log(card_clean$wage / 100)   # dollars

# Binary treatments
card_clean$somecol <- as.integer(card_clean$educ > 12)
card_clean$educ16  <- as.integer(card_clean$educ >= 16)

# Instrument
card_clean$Z_inst <- as.integer(card_clean$nearc4)

# expersq if missing
if (!"expersq" %in% names(card_clean)) {
  card_clean$expersq <- card_clean$exper^2
}

cat(sprintf("N = %d (should be 3,010)\n", nrow(card_clean)))
## N = 3010 (should be 3,010)
cat(sprintf("somecol mean = %.4f | educ16 mean = %.4f | nearc4 mean = %.4f\n",
            mean(card_clean$somecol), mean(card_clean$educ16),
            mean(card_clean$Z_inst)))
## somecol mean = 0.5053 | educ16 mean = 0.2714 | nearc4 mean = 0.6821
# Card spec — WITH intercept (for logit/CBPS)
X_card_kappa <- model.matrix(
  ~ exper + expersq + black + south + smsa + smsa66 +
    reg661 + reg662 + reg663 + reg664 + reg665 + reg666 + reg667 + reg668,
  data = card_clean
)
X_card_df <- as.data.frame(X_card_kappa[, -1, drop = FALSE])

# Kitagawa spec — WITH intercept
X_kit_kappa <- model.matrix(
  ~ black + south + smsa + smsa66 + south66,
  data = card_clean
)
X_kit_df <- as.data.frame(X_kit_kappa[, -1, drop = FALSE])

# Core vectors
Y_cnt <- card_clean$lwage_cnt
Y_dol <- card_clean$lwage
Z     <- card_clean$Z_inst
D1    <- card_clean$somecol   # treatment 1: some college
D2    <- card_clean$educ16    # treatment 2: college completion
k     <- log(100)

cat(sprintf("Card spec: %d covariates | Kitagawa spec: %d covariates\n",
            ncol(X_card_kappa) - 1, ncol(X_kit_kappa) - 1))
## Card spec: 14 covariates | Kitagawa spec: 5 covariates

Part I — SUW Table 3 Replication

Layout. SUW Table 3 has 8 columns: 2 treatments × 2 specifications × 2 outcome units. To keep things readable, results are split into two tabs by treatment (somecol / educ16). Within each tab, the four columns are spec × outcome unit. Normalized estimators (τ_cb_u, τ_ml_u, τ_ml_a10) are translation invariant — their cents and dollars columns should match. Unnormalized estimators (τ_ml_a, τ_ml_t, τ_ml_a0) are not.

# Run one full column: 2SLS + all six kappa estimators with analytical SEs
run_card_column <- function(Y, D, X_kappa, X_df) {
  list(
    tsls  = run_2sls(Y, D, Z, X_df, endog_name = "D"),
    kappa = kappa_analytic_se_all(Y, Z, D, X_kappa)
  )
}

# Treatment 1: somecol (cols 1-4)
col1 <- run_card_column(Y_cnt, D1, X_card_kappa, X_card_df)  # cents, Card
col2 <- run_card_column(Y_dol, D1, X_card_kappa, X_card_df)  # dollars, Card
col3 <- run_card_column(Y_cnt, D1, X_kit_kappa,  X_kit_df)   # cents, Kitagawa
col4 <- run_card_column(Y_dol, D1, X_kit_kappa,  X_kit_df)   # dollars, Kitagawa

# Treatment 2: educ16 (cols 5-8)
col5 <- run_card_column(Y_cnt, D2, X_card_kappa, X_card_df)  # cents, Card
col6 <- run_card_column(Y_dol, D2, X_card_kappa, X_card_df)  # dollars, Card
col7 <- run_card_column(Y_cnt, D2, X_kit_kappa,  X_kit_df)   # cents, Kitagawa
col8 <- run_card_column(Y_dol, D2, X_kit_kappa,  X_kit_df)   # dollars, Kitagawa

cat("All 8 columns computed.\n")
## All 8 columns computed.

Results

Treatment: Some College (educ > 12)

tbl_d1 <- build_treatment_table(col1, col2, col3, col4)

tbl_d1 %>%
  kbl(escape = FALSE, align = c("l","c","c","c","c"),
      caption = "SUW Table 3 — Treatment: somecol (educ > 12). Effect of some college on log wages.") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel A: 2SLS (HC1-robust)",          1, 1) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel B: Normalized kappa estimators", 2, 4) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel C: Unnormalized kappa estimators",5, 7) %>%
  row_spec(2:4, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  row_spec(5:7, background = "#fffbf0") %>%
  footnote(general = "Each cell: estimate (SE below). Blue = normalized (translation invariant). Orange = unnormalized. Card spec: exper, expersq, black, south, smsa, smsa66, reg661–668. Kitagawa spec: black, south, smsa, smsa66, south66. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.")

Treatment: College Completion (educ ≥ 16)

tbl_d2 <- build_treatment_table(col5, col6, col7, col8)

tbl_d2 %>%
  kbl(escape = FALSE, align = c("l","c","c","c","c"),
      caption = "SUW Table 3 — Treatment: educ16 (educ ≥ 16). Effect of college completion on log wages.") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel A: 2SLS (HC1-robust)",          1, 1) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel B: Normalized kappa estimators", 2, 4) %>%
  pack_rows("Panel C: Unnormalized kappa estimators",5, 7) %>%
  row_spec(2:4, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  row_spec(5:7, background = "#fffbf0") %>%
  footnote(general = "Each cell: estimate (SE below). Blue = normalized (translation invariant). Orange = unnormalized. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.")

Part II — Translation Invariance Check

Test. Cents minus dollars adds \(k = \log(100) \approx 4.605\) to every outcome. For any estimator: \(\hat\tau(\text{cents}) - \hat\tau(\text{dollars}) = k \cdot \sum_i \omega_i\). Normalized estimators have \(\sum_i \omega_i = 0\) (Match = TRUE); unnormalized do not. The four design cells (treatment × spec) are shown as tabs. The Card spec typically shows the largest failures for unnormalized estimators.

By Treatment & Specification

somecol — Card

style_ti(build_ti_table(D1, X_card_kappa),
         "Translation Invariance — somecol, Card spec")
Translation Invariance — somecol, Card spec
Estimator Dollars Cents Diff_actual Diff_pred Match
τ_cb_u 0.3762 0.3762 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_u 0.3308 0.3308 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_a10 0.3460 0.3460 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_a 0.1695 -0.3193 -0.488839 -0.488839 TRUE
τ_ml_t 0.1707 -0.3214 -0.492106 -0.492106 TRUE
τ_ml_a0 0.1542 -0.2904 -0.444597 -0.444597 TRUE
Note:
Diff_actual = Cents − Dollars. Diff_pred = Σωᵢ × log(100). Green = translation invariant.

somecol — Kitagawa

style_ti(build_ti_table(D1, X_kit_kappa),
         "Translation Invariance — somecol, Kitagawa spec")
Translation Invariance — somecol, Kitagawa spec
Estimator Dollars Cents Diff_actual Diff_pred Match
τ_cb_u 0.3310 0.3310 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_u 0.3556 0.3556 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_a10 0.2930 0.2930 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_a 0.8418 2.2476 1.405803 1.405803 TRUE
τ_ml_t 0.7688 2.0526 1.283819 1.283819 TRUE
τ_ml_a0 1.0659 2.8460 1.780059 1.780059 TRUE
Note:
Diff_actual = Cents − Dollars. Diff_pred = Σωᵢ × log(100). Green = translation invariant.

         "Translation Invariance — educ16, Card spec")

Estimator Dollars Cents Diff_actual Diff_pred Match

Part III — Weight Diagnostics

Reading the table. Sum_w ≈ 0 confirms translation invariance. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ² is effective sample size — watch for very low ESS here, especially the Kitagawa spec, where a weak first stage can drive ESS toward 1. Pct_neg = share of negative weights. Max_abs_w = largest single weight.

By Treatment & Specification

somecol — Card

style_diag(build_diag_table(D1, X_card_kappa),
           "Weight Diagnostics — somecol, Card spec")
Weight Diagnostics — somecol, Card spec
Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
τ_cb_u (CBPS, norm) 0.0000000 3 31.8 0.086905
τ_ml_u (MLE, norm) 0.0000000 4 31.8 0.051920
τ_ml_a10 (MLE, norm) 0.0000000 4 31.8 0.054816
τ_ml_a (unnorm) -0.1061501 3 31.8 0.054452
τ_ml_t (unnorm) -0.1068595 3 31.8 0.054816
τ_ml_a0 (unnorm) -0.0965429 4 31.8 0.049524
Note:
Sum_w ≈ 0 ⟺ translation invariant. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ². Low ESS = extreme weight concentration.

somecol — Kitagawa

style_diag(build_diag_table(D1, X_kit_kappa),
           "Weight Diagnostics — somecol, Kitagawa spec")

τ_ml_a10 (MLE, norm) 0.0000000 3 31.8 0.052601
τ_ml_a (unnorm) 0.3052662 3 31.8 0.041541
τ_ml_t (unnorm) 0.2787776 4 31.8 0.037937
τ_ml_a0 (unnorm) 0.3865350 2 31.8 0.052601

Sum_w ≈ 0 ⟺ translation invariant. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ². Low ESS = extreme weight concentration.

Combined Overview

combine_diag <- function() {
  cells <- list(
    list(D1, X_card_kappa, "somecol", "Card"),
    list(D1, X_kit_kappa,  "somecol", "Kitagawa"),
    list(D2, X_card_kappa, "educ16",  "Card"),
    list(D2, X_kit_kappa,  "educ16",  "Kitagawa")
  )
  out <- list()
  for (c in cells) {
    d <- build_diag_table(c[[1]], c[[2]])
    d$Treatment <- c[[3]]
    d$Spec      <- c[[4]]
    out[[length(out)+1]] <- d
  }
  res <- do.call(rbind, out)
  res[, c("Treatment","Spec","Estimator","Sum_w","ESS","Pct_neg","Max_abs_w")]
}

comb <- combine_diag()
comb %>%
  kbl(caption = "Weight Diagnostics — All 4 Cells (ESS focus)",
      align = c("l","l","l","r","r","r","r")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 12) %>%
  pack_rows("somecol — Card",      1, 6) %>%
  pack_rows("somecol — Kitagawa",  7, 12) %>%
  pack_rows("educ16 — Card",       13, 18) %>%
  pack_rows("educ16 — Kitagawa",   19, 24) %>%
  footnote(general = "Note the low ESS in the Kitagawa cells — a key discussion point: weak instrument destabilizes the kappa weights.")

somecol Kitagawa τ_ml_t (unnorm) 0.2787776 4 31.8 0.037937

educ16 Card τ_ml_u (MLE, norm) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.097168
educ16 Card τ_ml_a10 (MLE, norm) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.102551

educ16 Kitagawa τ_cb_u (CBPS, norm) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.081202
educ16 Kitagawa τ_ml_u (MLE, norm) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.078334
educ16 Kitagawa τ_ml_a10 (MLE, norm) 0.0000000 0 31.8 0.133832

educ16 Kitagawa τ_ml_a0 (unnorm) 0.9834616 0 31.8 0.133832
Note:
Note the low ESS in the Kitagawa cells — a key discussion point: weak instrument destabilizes the kappa weights.

Part IV — Algebraic Identity Check

Sanity check. Every kappa estimator satisfies \(\sum_i \omega_i Y_i = \hat\tau\) to machine precision. If any check fails, the weight construction is wrong. All should read TRUE.

identity_row <- function(D, X_kappa, Y, treatment, spec, unit) {
  p_ml  <- logit_mle(Z, X_kappa)
  p_cb  <- get_cbps_p(Z, X_kappa)
  kw_ml <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_ml)
  kw_cb <- kappa_outcome_weights(Z, D, p_cb)

  checks <- c(
    check_weight_identity(kw_cb$w_u,   Y, tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_cb)),
    check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_u,   Y, tau_u(Y, Z, D, p_ml)),
    check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a10, Y, tau_a10(Y, Z, D, p_ml)),
    check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a,   Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a")),
    check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a1,  Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a1")),
    check_weight_identity(kw_ml$w_a0,  Y, tau_unnorm(Y, Z, D, p_ml, "a0"))
  )
  data.frame(
    Treatment = treatment, Spec = spec, Unit = unit,
    `All 6 pass` = all(checks),
    `# passing`  = sprintf("%d / 6", sum(checks)),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

id_tbl <- do.call(rbind, list(
  identity_row(D1, X_card_kappa, Y_cnt, "somecol", "Card",     "cents"),
  identity_row(D1, X_card_kappa, Y_dol, "somecol", "Card",     "dollars"),
  identity_row(D1, X_kit_kappa,  Y_cnt, "somecol", "Kitagawa", "cents"),
  identity_row(D1, X_kit_kappa,  Y_dol, "somecol", "Kitagawa", "dollars"),
  identity_row(D2, X_card_kappa, Y_cnt, "educ16",  "Card",     "cents"),
  identity_row(D2, X_card_kappa, Y_dol, "educ16",  "Card",     "dollars"),
  identity_row(D2, X_kit_kappa,  Y_cnt, "educ16",  "Kitagawa", "cents"),
  identity_row(D2, X_kit_kappa,  Y_dol, "educ16",  "Kitagawa", "dollars")
))

id_tbl %>%
  kbl(caption = "Algebraic Identity Check — ΣωᵢYᵢ = τ̂ across all 8 columns",
      align = c("l","l","l","c","c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  footnote(general = "All cells should read TRUE / 6 of 6. This verifies the outcome-weight representation is correct for every estimator.")
Algebraic Identity Check — ΣωᵢYᵢ = τ̂ across all 8 columns
Treatment Spec Unit All 6 pass # passing
somecol Card cents TRUE 6 / 6
somecol Card dollars TRUE 6 / 6
somecol Kitagawa cents TRUE 6 / 6
somecol Kitagawa dollars TRUE 6 / 6
educ16 Card cents TRUE 6 / 6
educ16 Card dollars TRUE 6 / 6
educ16 Kitagawa cents TRUE 6 / 6
educ16 Kitagawa dollars TRUE 6 / 6
Note:
All cells should read TRUE / 6 of 6. This verifies the outcome-weight representation is correct for every estimator.

Part V — Double ML Extension (PLR-IV & Wald-AIPW)

Setup. dml_with_smoother() from the OutcomeWeights package (Knaus 2024), 5-fold cross-fitting, tune.parameters = "all". Four DML fits: two specifications (Card / Kitagawa) × two treatments (somecol / educ16), all on log wages in dollars. For each fit we report PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW. DML is run on dollars only — both estimators are translation invariant by construction (\(\sum_i \omega_i = 0\)), so the cents/dollars comparison would only confirm what holds algebraically.

library(OutcomeWeights)
library(cobalt)
library(viridis)
library(gridExtra)
library(ggplot2)

# DML covariate matrices — no intercept, attributes stripped for grf (make_X
# from functions_all.R). Same variables as the kappa matrices minus intercept.
X_dml_card <- make_X(
  ~ 0 + exper + expersq + black + south + smsa + smsa66 +
    reg661 + reg662 + reg663 + reg664 + reg665 + reg666 + reg667 + reg668,
  data = card_clean
)
X_dml_kit <- make_X(
  ~ 0 + black + south + smsa + smsa66 + south66,
  data = card_clean
)

cat(sprintf("X_dml_card: %dx%d | X_dml_kit: %dx%d\n",
            nrow(X_dml_card), ncol(X_dml_card),
            nrow(X_dml_kit),  ncol(X_dml_kit)))
## X_dml_card: 3010x14 | X_dml_kit: 3010x5

DML Estimation — 4 Fits

# Four DML fits: (Card, Kitagawa) x (somecol, educ16), all on Y_dol.
set.seed(42)
dml_card_d1 <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D1, X_dml_card, Z, n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
set.seed(42)
dml_card_d2 <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D2, X_dml_card, Z, n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
set.seed(42)
dml_kit_d1  <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D1, X_dml_kit,  Z, n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")
set.seed(42)
dml_kit_d2  <- dml_with_smoother(Y_dol, D2, X_dml_kit,  Z, n_cf_folds = 5, tune.parameters = "all")

res_card_d1 <- summary(dml_card_d1); res_card_d2 <- summary(dml_card_d2)
##           Estimate       SE       t       p    
## PLR       0.239084 0.017647 13.5482 < 2e-16 ***
## PLR-IV    0.680717 0.288671  2.3581 0.01843 *  
## AIPW-ATE       NaN       NA     NaN     NaN    
## Wald-AIPW 0.279284 0.214392  1.3027 0.19278    
## AIPW-ATT       NaN       NA     NaN     NaN    
## AIPW-ATU  0.227007 0.019826 11.4501 < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
##           Estimate       SE       t       p    
## PLR       0.319529 0.018839 16.9612 < 2e-16 ***
## PLR-IV    1.139922 0.535281  2.1296 0.03329 *  
## AIPW-ATE  0.342322 0.015054 22.7398 < 2e-16 ***
## Wald-AIPW 0.530289 0.397728  1.3333 0.18254    
## AIPW-ATT  0.238301 0.019234 12.3897 < 2e-16 ***
## AIPW-ATU  0.381075 0.015771 24.1637 < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
res_kit_d1  <- summary(dml_kit_d1);  res_kit_d2  <- summary(dml_kit_d2)
##           Estimate       SE      t         p    
## PLR       0.112698 0.015081 7.4729 1.022e-13 ***
## PLR-IV    0.592544 0.340076 1.7424   0.08154 .  
## AIPW-ATE  0.116735 0.015018 7.7730 1.044e-14 ***
## Wald-AIPW 0.292078 0.248921 1.1734   0.24074    
## AIPW-ATT  0.108982 0.015535 7.0154 2.820e-12 ***
## AIPW-ATU  0.124655 0.015908 7.8359 6.405e-15 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
##           Estimate       SE      t         p    
## PLR       0.152182 0.017650 8.6220 < 2.2e-16 ***
## PLR-IV    1.030285 0.683563 1.5072    0.1319    
## AIPW-ATE  0.172489 0.017927 9.6218 < 2.2e-16 ***
## Wald-AIPW 0.534740 0.462037 1.1574    0.2472    
## AIPW-ATT  0.147088 0.017984 8.1789 4.175e-16 ***
## AIPW-ATU  0.181952 0.018986 9.5834 < 2.2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
# Extract PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW outcome weights for every fit
extract_w <- function(dml_obj) {
  om <- get_outcome_weights(dml_obj)
  check_omega_rows(om)
  list(plriv    = as.vector(om$omega["PLR-IV",    ]),
       waldaipw = as.vector(om$omega["Wald-AIPW", ]))
}
w_card_d1 <- extract_w(dml_card_d1); w_card_d2 <- extract_w(dml_card_d2)
w_kit_d1  <- extract_w(dml_kit_d1);  w_kit_d2  <- extract_w(dml_kit_d2)

cat("All 4 DML fits complete.\n")
## All 4 DML fits complete.

Card — somecol

show_dml_cell(res_card_d1, w_card_d1, Y_dol,
              "DML — Card spec, somecol (some college), Y = log wages (dollars)")
DML — Card spec, somecol (some college), Y = log wages (dollars)
Estimator Estimate SE t p ΣωY = τ̂
PLR-IV 0.6807 0.2887 2.3581 0.0184 TRUE
Wald-AIPW 0.2793 0.2144 1.3027 0.1928 TRUE
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

Card — educ16

show_dml_cell(res_card_d2, w_card_d2, Y_dol,
              "DML — Card spec, educ16 (college completion), Y = log wages (dollars)")
DML — Card spec, educ16 (college completion), Y = log wages (dollars)
Estimator Estimate SE t p ΣωY = τ̂
PLR-IV 1.1399 0.5353 2.1296 0.0333 TRUE
Wald-AIPW 0.5303 0.3977 1.3333 0.1825 TRUE
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

Kitagawa — somecol

show_dml_cell(res_kit_d1, w_kit_d1, Y_dol,
              "DML — Kitagawa spec, somecol (some college), Y = log wages (dollars)")
DML — Kitagawa spec, somecol (some college), Y = log wages (dollars)
Estimator Estimate SE t p ΣωY = τ̂
PLR-IV 0.5925 0.3401 1.7424 0.0815 TRUE
Wald-AIPW 0.2921 0.2489 1.1734 0.2407 TRUE
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

Kitagawa — educ16

show_dml_cell(res_kit_d2, w_kit_d2, Y_dol,
              "DML — Kitagawa spec, educ16 (college completion), Y = log wages (dollars)")
DML — Kitagawa spec, educ16 (college completion), Y = log wages (dollars)
Estimator Estimate SE t p ΣωY = τ̂
PLR-IV 1.0303 0.6836 1.5072 0.1319 TRUE
Wald-AIPW 0.5347 0.4620 1.1574 0.2472 TRUE
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

DML vs. Kappa — Point Estimate Summary

# Kappa estimates (dollars) for all 4 cells — from functions_all.R
kappa_card_d1 <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D1, X_card_kappa)
kappa_card_d2 <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D2, X_card_kappa)
kappa_kit_d1  <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D1, X_kit_kappa)
kappa_kit_d2  <- kappa_analytic_se_all(Y_dol, Z, D2, X_kit_kappa)

cell <- function(coef, se) {
  if (is.na(coef)) return("—")
  if (is.na(se) || !is.finite(se) || se <= 0)
    return(sprintf("%.3f (NA)", coef))
  pval  <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(coef / se))
  stars <- ifelse(pval < 0.01, "***", ifelse(pval < 0.05, "**",
                  ifelse(pval < 0.10, "*", "")))
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", coef, stars, se)
}

dml_cell <- function(res, est) {
  coef_val <- get_estimate(res, est)
  se_val   <- if ("SE" %in% colnames(res)) as.numeric(res[est, "SE"])
              else if ("Std. Error" %in% colnames(res)) as.numeric(res[est, "Std. Error"])
              else NA_real_
  cell(coef_val, se_val)
}

# DML rows
dml_rows <- rbind(
  data.frame(Estimator = "PLR-IV (DML)",
             `somecol / Card`     = dml_cell(res_card_d1, "PLR-IV"),
             `somecol / Kitagawa` = dml_cell(res_kit_d1,  "PLR-IV"),
             `educ16 / Card`      = dml_cell(res_card_d2, "PLR-IV"),
             `educ16 / Kitagawa`  = dml_cell(res_kit_d2,  "PLR-IV"),
             check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  data.frame(Estimator = "Wald-AIPW (DML)",
             `somecol / Card`     = dml_cell(res_card_d1, "Wald-AIPW"),
             `somecol / Kitagawa` = dml_cell(res_kit_d1,  "Wald-AIPW"),
             `educ16 / Card`      = dml_cell(res_card_d2, "Wald-AIPW"),
             `educ16 / Kitagawa`  = dml_cell(res_kit_d2,  "Wald-AIPW"),
             check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
)

# Kappa rows
ests   <- c("tau_cb_u","tau_ml_u","tau_ml_a10","tau_ml_a","tau_ml_t","tau_ml_a0")
labels <- c("τ_cb_u","τ_ml_u","τ_ml_a10","τ_ml_a","τ_ml_t","τ_ml_a0")
kappa_rows <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(ests), function(i) {
  e <- ests[i]
  data.frame(
    Estimator = labels[i],
    `somecol / Card`     = cell(kappa_card_d1$estimates[e], kappa_card_d1$se[e]),
    `somecol / Kitagawa` = cell(kappa_kit_d1$estimates[e],  kappa_kit_d1$se[e]),
    `educ16 / Card`      = cell(kappa_card_d2$estimates[e], kappa_card_d2$se[e]),
    `educ16 / Kitagawa`  = cell(kappa_kit_d2$estimates[e],  kappa_kit_d2$se[e]),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}))

summary_tbl <- rbind(dml_rows, kappa_rows)

summary_tbl %>%
  kbl(align = c("l","c","c","c","c"),
      caption = "DML vs. Kappa — Point Estimates (SE), Y = log wages (dollars)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  pack_rows("Double ML", 1, 2) %>%
  pack_rows("Kappa — normalized", 3, 5) %>%
  pack_rows("Kappa — unnormalized", 6, 8) %>%
  row_spec(1:2, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  row_spec(6:8, background = "#fffbf0") %>%
  footnote(general = "Each cell: estimate (SE). Blue = DML. Orange = unnormalized kappa. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.")
DML vs. Kappa — Point Estimates (SE), Y = log wages (dollars)
Estimator somecol / Card somecol / Kitagawa educ16 / Card educ16 / Kitagawa
Double ML
PLR-IV (DML) 0.681** (0.289) 0.593* (0.340) 1.140** (0.535) 1.030 (0.684)
Wald-AIPW (DML) 0.279 (0.214) 0.292 (0.249) 0.530 (0.398) 0.535 (0.462)
Kappa — normalized
τ_cb_u 0.376* (0.223) 0.331 (0.236) 0.853 (0.549) 0.588 (0.433)
τ_ml_u 0.331 (0.202) 0.356 (0.244) 0.619 (0.387) 0.628 (0.448)
τ_ml_a10 0.346* (0.200) 0.293 (0.252) 0.586* (0.356) 0.836 (0.821)
Kappa — unnormalized
τ_ml_a 0.170 (0.370) 0.842** (0.362) 0.315 (0.696) 1.617* (0.891)
τ_ml_t 0.171 (0.367) 0.769** (0.308) 0.319 (0.687) 1.367** (0.648)
τ_ml_a0 0.154 (0.354) 1.066* (0.574) 0.266 (0.639) 2.712 (2.577)
Note:
Each cell: estimate (SE). Blue = DML. Orange = unnormalized kappa. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.

DML Translation Invariance

Test. For the DML weights, \(\hat\tau(\text{cents}) - \hat\tau(\text{dollars}) = \log(100) \cdot \sum_i \omega_i\). PLR-IV and Wald-AIPW should satisfy \(\sum_i \omega_i = 0\) (Match = TRUE) in all four cells.

dml_ti <- do.call(rbind, list(
  doubleml_translation_row("PLR-IV (Card, somecol)",        w_card_d1$plriv,    Y_dol, Y_cnt, k),
  doubleml_translation_row("Wald-AIPW (Card, somecol)",     w_card_d1$waldaipw, Y_dol, Y_cnt, k),
  doubleml_translation_row("PLR-IV (Card, educ16)",         w_card_d2$plriv,    Y_dol, Y_cnt, k),
  doubleml_translation_row("Wald-AIPW (Card, educ16)",      w_card_d2$waldaipw, Y_dol, Y_cnt, k),
  doubleml_translation_row("PLR-IV (Kitagawa, somecol)",    w_kit_d1$plriv,     Y_dol, Y_cnt, k),
  doubleml_translation_row("Wald-AIPW (Kitagawa, somecol)", w_kit_d1$waldaipw,  Y_dol, Y_cnt, k),
  doubleml_translation_row("PLR-IV (Kitagawa, educ16)",     w_kit_d2$plriv,     Y_dol, Y_cnt, k),
  doubleml_translation_row("Wald-AIPW (Kitagawa, educ16)",  w_kit_d2$waldaipw,  Y_dol, Y_cnt, k)
))

dml_ti %>%
  kbl(caption = "DML Translation Invariance — all 4 fits (PLR-IV & Wald-AIPW)",
      align = c("l","r","r","r","r","c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  row_spec(which(dml_ti$Match),  background = "#eafbea") %>%
  row_spec(which(!dml_ti$Match), background = "#fff0f0") %>%
  footnote(general = "Diff_actual = Cents − Dollars. Diff_pred = Σωᵢ × log(100). Green = translation invariant.")
DML Translation Invariance — all 4 fits (PLR-IV & Wald-AIPW)
Estimator Dollars Cents Diff_actual Diff_pred Match
PLR-IV (Card, somecol) 0.6807 0.6807 0 0 TRUE
Wald-AIPW (Card, somecol) 0.2793 0.2793 0 0 TRUE
PLR-IV (Card, educ16) 1.1399 1.1399 0 0 TRUE
Wald-AIPW (Card, educ16) 0.5303 0.5303 0 0 TRUE
PLR-IV (Kitagawa, somecol) 0.5925 0.5925 0 0 TRUE
Wald-AIPW (Kitagawa, somecol) 0.2921 0.2921 0 0 TRUE
PLR-IV (Kitagawa, educ16) 1.0303 1.0303 0 0 TRUE
Wald-AIPW (Kitagawa, educ16) 0.5347 0.5347 0 0 TRUE
Note:
Diff_actual = Cents − Dollars. Diff_pred = Σωᵢ × log(100). Green = translation invariant.

DML Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa

Reading the table. Sum_w ≈ 0 confirms translation invariance. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ² is effective sample size — compare DML vs. kappa, and Card vs. Kitagawa. Pct_neg and Max_abs_w flag weight concentration.

Card — somecol

style_dml_diag(dml_diag_cell(w_card_d1, kw_card_d1),
               "Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | Card, somecol")
Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | Card, somecol
Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
PLR-IV (DML) 0.0000000 2 33.1 0.032653
Wald-AIPW (DML) 0.0000000 3 31.8 0.145639
τ_cb_u (kappa) 0.0000000 3 31.8 0.086905
τ_ml_u (kappa) 0.0000000 4 31.8 0.051920
τ_ml_a10 (kappa) 0.0000000 4 31.8 0.054816
τ_ml_a (kappa) -0.1061501 3 31.8 0.054452
τ_ml_t/a1 (kappa) -0.1068595 3 31.8 0.054816
τ_ml_a0 (kappa) -0.0965429 4 31.8 0.049524
Note:
Blue = DML. Sum_w ≈ 0 ⟺ translation invariant. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ².

Card — educ16

style_dml_diag(dml_diag_cell(w_card_d2, kw_card_d2),
               "Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | Card, educ16")
Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | Card, educ16
Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w

Wald-AIPW (DML) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.276531
τ_cb_u (kappa) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.197058
τ_ml_u (kappa) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.097168
τ_ml_a10 (kappa) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.102551
τ_ml_a (kappa) -0.1974461 1 31.8 0.101285
τ_ml_t/a1 (kappa) -0.1999148 1 31.8 0.102551
τ_ml_a0 (kappa) -0.1666075 1 31.8 0.085466
Note:
Blue = DML. Sum_w ≈ 0 ⟺ translation invariant. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ².

Kitagawa — somecol

style_dml_diag(dml_diag_cell(w_kit_d1, kw_kit_d1),
               "Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | Kitagawa, somecol")

τ_ml_u (kappa) 0.0000000 3 31.8 0.044385

τ_ml_a (kappa) 0.3052662 3 31.8 0.041541

Kitagawa — educ16

style_dml_diag(dml_diag_cell(w_kit_d2, kw_kit_d2),
               "Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | Kitagawa, educ16")
Weight Diagnostics — DML vs. Kappa | Kitagawa, educ16
Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w

Wald-AIPW (DML) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.119277

Love Plots — Covariate Balance

Reading Love plots. Absolute standardized mean differences (ASMD) using signed outcome weights with the \((2D_i - 1)\) sign flip (Knaus 2024). Dashed line at ASMD = 0.10; lower = better balance. Shown for the Card specification (richest covariate set, where estimator differences are most visible), one panel per treatment.

Card spec — somecol (all 8 estimators)

lp_d1 <- list(
  make_love("PLR-IV (DML)",     w_card_d1$plriv,     D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("Wald-AIPW (DML)",  w_card_d1$waldaipw,  D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_cb_u (CBPS)",    kw_card_d1$tau_cb_u, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_u",           kw_card_d1$tau_ml_u, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_a10",         kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a10, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_a (unnorm)",  kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_t (unnorm)",  kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a1, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_a0 (unnorm)", kw_card_d1$tau_ml_a0, D1, X_dml_card)
)
grid.arrange(grobs = lp_d1, nrow = 2,
             top = "Love Plots — Card spec, somecol: DML vs. Kappa")

Card spec — educ16 (all 8 estimators)

lp_d2 <- list(
  make_love("PLR-IV (DML)",     w_card_d2$plriv,     D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("Wald-AIPW (DML)",  w_card_d2$waldaipw,  D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_cb_u (CBPS)",    kw_card_d2$tau_cb_u, D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_u",           kw_card_d2$tau_ml_u, D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_a10",         kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a10, D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_a (unnorm)",  kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a, D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_t (unnorm)",  kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a1, D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_a0 (unnorm)", kw_card_d2$tau_ml_a0, D2, X_dml_card)
)
grid.arrange(grobs = lp_d2, nrow = 2,
             top = "Love Plots — Card spec, educ16: DML vs. Kappa")

DML only — Card vs. Kitagawa (somecol)

lp_cmp <- list(
  make_love("PLR-IV (Card)",        w_card_d1$plriv,    D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("Wald-AIPW (Card)",     w_card_d1$waldaipw, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("PLR-IV (Kitagawa)",    w_kit_d1$plriv,     D1, X_dml_kit),
  make_love("Wald-AIPW (Kitagawa)", w_kit_d1$waldaipw,  D1, X_dml_kit)
)
grid.arrange(grobs = lp_cmp, nrow = 1,
             top = "Love Plots — DML: Card vs. Kitagawa spec (somecol)")

End of Card framework presentation (kappa replication + DML extension).
All functions sourced from functions_all.R. No estimation function defined inline — only thin table/plot-display wrappers.

Part VI — DoubleML Learner Comparison (Linear / Ranger / XGBoost)

Setup. Three learners via DoubleMLIIVM (dev version of OutcomeWeights required for get_outcome_weights() on R6 objects), 5-fold cross-fitting, score = "LATE". Each learner is fit on all 4 cells (Card / Kitagawa × somecol / educ16) — 12 IIVM fits total. Learners: (1) linear + logistic (parametric baseline), (2) ranger random forest (keep.inbag = TRUE, required for the affine smoother identity), (3) XGBoost with the affine-smoother hyperparameters (alpha=0, subsample=1, max_delta_step=0, base_score=0 on the regression learner). The identity \(\sum_i \omega_i Y_i = \hat\tau\) is checked for each — XGBoost may fail it under XGBoost ≥ 1.6 due to the base_score change.

library(DoubleML)
library(mlr3verse)

# Small SE accessor for DoubleMLIIVM summary (the only helper not in
# functions_all.R). Everything else — get_dml_coef, check_doubleml_identity,
# doubleml_translation_row, make_X, kappa_weights_bundle, make_love, weight_diag
# — is sourced from functions_all.R.
get_dml_se <- function(obj) {
  s <- obj$summary()
  if ("std err"    %in% colnames(s)) return(as.numeric(s[1, "std err"]))
  if ("Std. Error" %in% colnames(s)) return(as.numeric(s[1, "Std. Error"]))
  NA_real_
}

# DoubleMLData objects — one per cell. d and z must be numeric 0/1.
make_dml_data <- function(D, X_dml) {
  df <- as.data.frame(X_dml)
  df$y <- as.numeric(Y_dol)
  df$d <- as.numeric(D)
  df$z <- as.numeric(Z)
  DoubleMLData$new(data = df, y_col = "y", d_cols = "d", z_cols = "z",
                   x_cols = colnames(X_dml))
}

dat_card_d1 <- make_dml_data(D1, X_dml_card)
dat_card_d2 <- make_dml_data(D2, X_dml_card)
dat_kit_d1  <- make_dml_data(D1, X_dml_kit)
dat_kit_d2  <- make_dml_data(D2, X_dml_kit)

cat("DoubleML data objects built for all 4 cells.\n")
## DoubleML data objects built for all 4 cells.

Estimation — 3 Learners × 4 Cells

Linear + Logistic

lm_card_d1 <- fit_iivm(dat_card_d1, ml_lm(), Y_dol)
lm_card_d2 <- fit_iivm(dat_card_d2, ml_lm(), Y_dol)
lm_kit_d1  <- fit_iivm(dat_kit_d1,  ml_lm(), Y_dol)
lm_kit_d2  <- fit_iivm(dat_kit_d2,  ml_lm(), Y_dol)

data.frame(
  Cell        = c("Card / somecol","Card / educ16","Kitagawa / somecol","Kitagawa / educ16"),
  Estimate    = round(c(get_dml_coef(lm_card_d1$obj), get_dml_coef(lm_card_d2$obj),
                        get_dml_coef(lm_kit_d1$obj),  get_dml_coef(lm_kit_d2$obj)), 4),
  SE          = round(c(get_dml_se(lm_card_d1$obj), get_dml_se(lm_card_d2$obj),
                        get_dml_se(lm_kit_d1$obj),  get_dml_se(lm_kit_d2$obj)), 4),
  `ΣωY = τ̂` = c(lm_card_d1$ok, lm_card_d2$ok, lm_kit_d1$ok, lm_kit_d2$ok),
  check.names = FALSE
) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Wald-AIPW — Linear + Logistic (all 4 cells)",
      align = c("l","r","r","c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE, font_size = 13) %>%
  footnote(general = "ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).")
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.3991     0.2483   1.608    0.108
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.8843     0.6079   1.455    0.146
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.3112     0.2351   1.324    0.186
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.5336     0.4092   1.304    0.192
Wald-AIPW — Linear + Logistic (all 4 cells)
Cell Estimate SE ΣωY = τ̂
Card / somecol 0.3991 0.2483 TRUE
Card / educ16 0.8843 0.6079 TRUE
Kitagawa / somecol 0.3112 0.2351 TRUE
Kitagawa / educ16 0.5336 0.4092 TRUE
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).

Ranger

rf_card_d1 <- fit_iivm(dat_card_d1, ml_rf(), Y_dol)
rf_card_d2 <- fit_iivm(dat_card_d2, ml_rf(), Y_dol)
rf_kit_d1  <- fit_iivm(dat_kit_d1,  ml_rf(), Y_dol)
rf_kit_d2  <- fit_iivm(dat_kit_d2,  ml_rf(), Y_dol)

data.frame(
  Cell        = c("Card / somecol","Card / educ16","Kitagawa / somecol","Kitagawa / educ16"),
  Estimate    = round(c(get_dml_coef(rf_card_d1$obj), get_dml_coef(rf_card_d2$obj),
                        get_dml_coef(rf_kit_d1$obj),  get_dml_coef(rf_kit_d2$obj)), 4),
  SE          = round(c(get_dml_se(rf_card_d1$obj), get_dml_se(rf_card_d2$obj),
                        get_dml_se(rf_kit_d1$obj),  get_dml_se(rf_kit_d2$obj)), 4),
  `ΣωY = τ̂` = c(rf_card_d1$ok, rf_card_d2$ok, rf_kit_d1$ok, rf_kit_d2$ok),
  check.names = FALSE
) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Wald-AIPW — Ranger (all 4 cells)",
      align = c("l","r","r","c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE, font_size = 13) %>%
  footnote(general = "ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check (must be TRUE).")
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.2621     0.1948   1.345    0.179
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.4926     0.3751   1.313    0.189
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.2943     0.2310   1.274    0.203

Wald-AIPW — Ranger (all 4 cells)
Cell Estimate SE ΣωY = τ̂
Card / somecol 0.2621 0.1948 TRUE
Card / educ16 0.4926 0.3751 TRUE
Kitagawa / somecol 0.2943 0.2310 TRUE
Kitagawa / educ16 0.5142 0.4084 TRUE
Note:

XGBoost

xgb_card_d1 <- fit_iivm(dat_card_d1, ml_xgb(), Y_dol)
xgb_card_d2 <- fit_iivm(dat_card_d2, ml_xgb(), Y_dol)
xgb_kit_d1  <- fit_iivm(dat_kit_d1,  ml_xgb(), Y_dol)
xgb_kit_d2  <- fit_iivm(dat_kit_d2,  ml_xgb(), Y_dol)

data.frame(
  Cell        = c("Card / somecol","Card / educ16","Kitagawa / somecol","Kitagawa / educ16"),
  Estimate    = round(c(get_dml_coef(xgb_card_d1$obj), get_dml_coef(xgb_card_d2$obj),
                        get_dml_coef(xgb_kit_d1$obj),  get_dml_coef(xgb_kit_d2$obj)), 4),
  SE          = round(c(get_dml_se(xgb_card_d1$obj), get_dml_se(xgb_card_d2$obj),
                        get_dml_se(xgb_kit_d1$obj),  get_dml_se(xgb_kit_d2$obj)), 4),
  `ΣωY = τ̂` = c(xgb_card_d1$ok, xgb_card_d2$ok, xgb_kit_d1$ok, xgb_kit_d2$ok),
  check.names = FALSE
) %>%
  kbl(caption = "Wald-AIPW — XGBoost (all 4 cells)",
      align = c("l","r","r","c")) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE, font_size = 13) %>%
  footnote(general = "ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check. May read FALSE for XGBoost ≥ 1.6 due to the base_score change (non-affine smoother).")
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    -13.42      14.41  -0.931    0.352
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    -3.198      4.054  -0.789     0.43
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d   -0.1134     0.6271  -0.181    0.857
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    -3.656    100.914  -0.036    0.971
Wald-AIPW — XGBoost (all 4 cells)
Cell Estimate SE ΣωY = τ̂
Card / somecol -13.4155 14.4111 FALSE
Card / educ16 -3.1980 4.0540 FALSE
Kitagawa / somecol -0.1134 0.6271 FALSE
Kitagawa / educ16 -3.6561 100.9143 FALSE
Note:
ΣωY = τ̂: algebraic identity check. May read FALSE for XGBoost ≥ 1.6 due to the base_score change (non-affine smoother).

Note on XGBoost. The algebraic identity \(\sum_i \omega_i Y_i = \hat\tau\) may fail for XGBoost because of a change in base_score behaviour in XGBoost ≥ 1.6, which breaks the affine smoother condition (Knaus & Rakov). Ranger is recommended for standard use; XGBoost results should be flagged where the identity does not hold.

Learner Comparison Table

ext_cell <- function(coef, se) {
  if (is.na(coef) || !is.finite(coef)) return("—")
  if (is.na(se) || !is.finite(se) || se <= 0) return(sprintf("%.3f (NA)", coef))
  pval  <- 2 * pnorm(-abs(coef / se))
  stars <- ifelse(pval < 0.01, "***", ifelse(pval < 0.05, "**",
                  ifelse(pval < 0.10, "*", "")))
  sprintf("%.3f%s (%.3f)", coef, stars, se)
}

learner_row <- function(label, f_card_d1, f_kit_d1, f_card_d2, f_kit_d2) {
  data.frame(
    Estimator = label,
    `somecol / Card`     = ext_cell(get_dml_coef(f_card_d1$obj), get_dml_se(f_card_d1$obj)),
    `somecol / Kitagawa` = ext_cell(get_dml_coef(f_kit_d1$obj),  get_dml_se(f_kit_d1$obj)),
    `educ16 / Card`      = ext_cell(get_dml_coef(f_card_d2$obj), get_dml_se(f_card_d2$obj)),
    `educ16 / Kitagawa`  = ext_cell(get_dml_coef(f_kit_d2$obj),  get_dml_se(f_kit_d2$obj)),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

# Kappa reference rows (tau_cb_u, tau_ml_u) for context
kappa_ref_row <- function(label, key) {
  data.frame(
    Estimator = label,
    `somecol / Card`     = ext_cell(kappa_card_d1$estimates[key], kappa_card_d1$se[key]),
    `somecol / Kitagawa` = ext_cell(kappa_kit_d1$estimates[key],  kappa_kit_d1$se[key]),
    `educ16 / Card`      = ext_cell(kappa_card_d2$estimates[key], kappa_card_d2$se[key]),
    `educ16 / Kitagawa`  = ext_cell(kappa_kit_d2$estimates[key],  kappa_kit_d2$se[key]),
    check.names = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}

ext_tbl <- rbind(
  learner_row("Wald-AIPW (linear+logit)", lm_card_d1,  lm_kit_d1,  lm_card_d2,  lm_kit_d2),
  learner_row("Wald-AIPW (ranger)",       rf_card_d1,  rf_kit_d1,  rf_card_d2,  rf_kit_d2),
  learner_row("Wald-AIPW (XGBoost)",      xgb_card_d1, xgb_kit_d1, xgb_card_d2, xgb_kit_d2),
  kappa_ref_row("τ_cb_u (kappa, ref)",  "tau_cb_u"),
  kappa_ref_row("τ_ml_u (kappa, ref)",  "tau_ml_u")
)
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables

##

##

##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.5336     0.4092   1.304    0.192
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.2621     0.1948   1.345    0.179
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.2943     0.2310   1.274    0.203
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.4926     0.3751   1.313    0.189
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    0.5142     0.4084   1.259    0.208
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    -13.42      14.41  -0.931    0.352
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d   -0.1134     0.6271  -0.181    0.857
## 
## 
## Estimates and significance testing of the effect of target variables
##   Estimate. Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## d    -3.198      4.054  -0.789     0.43

## d    -3.656    100.914  -0.036    0.971
ext_tbl %>%
  kbl(align = c("l","c","c","c","c"),
      caption = "Wald-AIPW Learner Comparison — DoubleML vs. Kappa (Y = log wages, dollars)") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped","hover","condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, font_size = 13) %>%
  pack_rows("DoubleML learners", 1, 3) %>%
  pack_rows("Kappa reference",   4, 5) %>%
  row_spec(1:3, background = "#eef6ff") %>%
  footnote(general = "Each cell: estimate (SE). Blue = DoubleML. All score='LATE', n_folds=5. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.")
Wald-AIPW Learner Comparison — DoubleML vs. Kappa (Y = log wages, dollars)
Estimator somecol / Card somecol / Kitagawa educ16 / Card educ16 / Kitagawa
DoubleML learners
Wald-AIPW (linear+logit) 0.399 (0.248) 0.311 (0.235) 0.884 (0.608) 0.534 (0.409)
Wald-AIPW (ranger) 0.262 (0.195) 0.294 (0.231) 0.493 (0.375) 0.514 (0.408)
Wald-AIPW (XGBoost) -13.416 (14.411) -0.113 (0.627) -3.198 (4.054) -3.656 (100.914)
Kappa reference
τ_cb_u (kappa, ref) 0.376* (0.223) 0.331 (0.236) 0.853 (0.549) 0.588 (0.433)
τ_ml_u (kappa, ref) 0.331 (0.202) 0.356 (0.244) 0.619 (0.387) 0.628 (0.448)
Note:
Each cell: estimate (SE). Blue = DoubleML. All score=‘LATE’, n_folds=5. Stars: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01.

Translation Invariance — Learners vs. Kappa

Test. \(\hat\tau(\text{cents}) - \hat\tau(\text{dollars}) = \log(100) \cdot \sum_i \omega_i\). All three DoubleML learners are translation invariant by construction (\(\sum_i \omega_i = 0\), Match = TRUE). The unnormalized kappa rows are shown for contrast.

Card — somecol

style_ext_ti(ext_ti_block(D1, X_card_kappa, lm_card_d1$w, rf_card_d1$w, xgb_card_d1$w),
             "Translation Invariance — Card, somecol")
Translation Invariance — Card, somecol
Estimator Dollars Cents Diff_actual Diff_pred Match
Wald-AIPW (linear+logit) 0.3991 0.3991 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
Wald-AIPW (ranger) 0.2621 0.2621 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
Wald-AIPW (XGBoost) -13.4155 -19.3134 -5.897860 -5.897860 TRUE
τ_cb_u (kappa) 0.3762 0.3762 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_u (kappa) 0.3308 0.3308 0.000000 0.000000 TRUE
τ_ml_a (unnorm) 0.1695 -0.3193 -0.488839 -0.488839 TRUE
τ_ml_t (unnorm) 0.1707 -0.3214 -0.492106 -0.492106 TRUE
τ_ml_a0 (unnorm) 0.1542 -0.2904 -0.444597 -0.444597 TRUE
Note:
Diff_actual = Cents − Dollars. Diff_pred = Σωᵢ × log(100). Green = translation invariant. Bold = DoubleML learners.

Card — educ16

style_ext_ti(ext_ti_block(D2, X_card_kappa, lm_card_d2$w, rf_card_d2$w, xgb_card_d2$w),
             "Translation Invariance — Card, educ16")

Weight Diagnostics — All Learners × 4 Cells

Reading the table. Sum_w ≈ 0 confirms translation invariance. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ² — compare across learners (do ranger and XGBoost diverge?) and across specs (does the richer Card spec lower ESS vs. Kitagawa?). Pct_neg, Max_abs_w flag weight concentration.

Card — somecol

style_ext_diag(ext_diag_block(D1, X_card_kappa, lm_card_d1$w, rf_card_d1$w, xgb_card_d1$w),
               "Weight Diagnostics — Card, somecol")
Weight Diagnostics — Card, somecol
Estimator Sum_w ESS Pct_neg Max_abs_w
Wald-AIPW (linear+logit) 0.0000000 3 31.6 0.080896
Wald-AIPW (ranger) 0.0000000 3 31.8 0.135400
Wald-AIPW (XGBoost) -1.2807040 0 57.0 20.028391
τ_cb_u (kappa, CBPS) 0.0000000 3 31.8 0.086905
τ_ml_u (kappa, MLE) 0.0000000 4 31.8 0.051920
τ_ml_a10 (kappa, MLE) 0.0000000 4 31.8 0.054816
τ_ml_a (kappa, unnorm) -0.1061501 3 31.8 0.054452
τ_ml_t (kappa, unnorm) -0.1068595 3 31.8 0.054816
τ_ml_a0 (kappa, unnorm) -0.0965429 4 31.8 0.049524
Note:
Blue = DoubleML learners. Sum_w ≈ 0 ⟺ translation invariant. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ².

Card — educ16

               "Weight Diagnostics — Card, educ16")

Wald-AIPW (ranger) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.099308
Wald-AIPW (XGBoost) -1.9959090 0 31.5 22.039408
τ_cb_u (kappa, CBPS) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.081202
τ_ml_u (kappa, MLE) 0.0000000 1 31.8 0.078334
τ_ml_a10 (kappa, MLE) 0.0000000 0 31.8 0.133832
τ_ml_a (kappa, unnorm) 0.5863184 1 31.8 0.079788
τ_ml_t (kappa, unnorm) 0.4958309 1 31.8 0.067474
τ_ml_a0 (kappa, unnorm) 0.9834616 0 31.8 0.133832
Note:
Blue = DoubleML learners. Sum_w ≈ 0 ⟺ translation invariant. ESS = 1/Σωᵢ².

Love Plots — Learner Comparison

Reading Love plots. ASMD with the \((2D_i - 1)\) sign flip. Dashed line at 0.10. Shown for the Card spec (richest covariate set), one tab per treatment, plus a spec-comparison panel isolating the ranger learner across Card vs. Kitagawa.

Card — somecol (3 learners + 2 kappa)

kw_card_d1 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D1, X_card_kappa)
lp1 <- list(
  make_love("Linear+Logit\nCard, somecol",  lm_card_d1$w,        D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("Ranger\nCard, somecol",        rf_card_d1$w,        D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("XGBoost\nCard, somecol",       xgb_card_d1$w,       D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_cb_u\nCard, somecol",        kw_card_d1$tau_cb_u, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_u\nCard, somecol",        kw_card_d1$tau_ml_u, D1, X_dml_card)
)
grid.arrange(grobs = lp1, nrow = 1,
             top = "Love Plots — DoubleML learners vs. kappa | Card, somecol")

Card — educ16 (3 learners + 2 kappa)

kw_card_d2 <- kappa_weights_bundle(Z, D2, X_card_kappa)
lp2 <- list(
  make_love("Linear+Logit\nCard, educ16",   lm_card_d2$w,        D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("Ranger\nCard, educ16",         rf_card_d2$w,        D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("XGBoost\nCard, educ16",        xgb_card_d2$w,       D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_cb_u\nCard, educ16",         kw_card_d2$tau_cb_u, D2, X_dml_card),
  make_love("τ_ml_u\nCard, educ16",         kw_card_d2$tau_ml_u, D2, X_dml_card)
)
grid.arrange(grobs = lp2, nrow = 1,
             top = "Love Plots — DoubleML learners vs. kappa | Card, educ16")

Spec Comparison — Card vs. Kitagawa (somecol)

lp3 <- list(
  make_love("Ranger\nCard spec",        rf_card_d1$w, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("Linear+Logit\nCard spec",  lm_card_d1$w, D1, X_dml_card),
  make_love("Ranger\nKitagawa spec",    rf_kit_d1$w,  D1, X_dml_kit),
  make_love("Linear+Logit\nKitagawa spec", lm_kit_d1$w, D1, X_dml_kit)
)
grid.arrange(grobs = lp3, nrow = 1,
             top = "Love Plots — Card vs. Kitagawa spec | DoubleML learners | somecol")

End of Card framework presentation (kappa replication + DML + learner comparison).
All functions sourced from functions_all.R; only get_dml_se and thin table/plot wrappers are defined inline.
